h1: GAF Timberline HDZ vs Owens Corning Duration Shingles: Side-by-Side Comparison (2026)
lead_summary: GAF Timberline HDZ leads on the WindProven unlimited wind warranty, color count, and install speed. Owens Corning Duration leads on the SureNail triple-layer nailing strip and a heavier feel that some contractors prefer. Algae coverage is now tied at 25 years. On material cost, GAF tends to run about $8 per square cheaper than Owens Corning at retail, but supply-house pricing varies by market.
choose_a: You want the WindProven limited wind warranty with no maximum wind speed cap (full GAF system install required).
You want the widest color library on the market (33 colors across HD and Bold Definition collections).
Your contractor is GAF Master Elite certified for the Golden Pledge lifetime warranty.
You value the wide StrikeZone nailing area for faster, more forgiving installs.
Home Depot is your closest retailer and you are sourcing materials locally.
choose_b: You want the SureNail triple-layer nailing strip your contractor prefers.
You buy through Lowe's or an Owens Corning Platinum supply house.
You prefer the muted, earthy color range (Driftwood, Sand Castle, Sand Dune).
Your contractor is an Owens Corning Platinum Preferred installer for the longest workmanship coverage.
You want a slightly heavier dimensional look at the same warranty tier.

comparison_table.winner_note: GAF $8 per square cheaper at retail

comparison_table.winner_note: HDZ stronger warranty terms with full-system install
Tie at base; Duration FLEX offers Class 4 upgrade path

comparison_table.a_ctx: Extends to 50yr non-prorated with Master Elite
comparison_table.winner_note: Both lifetime; contractor cert tier matters more than brand

cost_delta.notes: GAF Timberline HDZ runs ~$8/sq cheaper at retail. Installed delta is small unless the contractor's GAF-system requirements (matching starter, ridge, underlayment) add significant accessory cost. In contractor surveys, GAF often comes in 5 to 10 percent cheaper on the same 30-square home.

faq_items.q: Which is better, GAF or Owens Corning shingles?
faq_items.a: Both are top-tier architectural asphalt shingles. GAF wins on wind warranty terms and color count. Owens Corning wins on the SureNail nailing strip and slightly heavier feel. For most homeowners, contractor certification level matters more than which brand you choose.

faq_items.q: Are GAF shingles cheaper than Owens Corning?
faq_items.a: Generally yes. GAF Timberline HDZ typically runs $8 per square cheaper at retail than Owens Corning Duration. On a 30-square home, that is about $240 in material cost. Installed pricing varies by contractor labor and accessory choices.

faq_items.q: Which brand has better wind protection?
faq_items.a: GAF, when installed as a full GAF system. The WindProven Limited Wind Warranty has no maximum wind speed cap, which is unmatched in the asphalt category. Both shingles are 130 mph rated out of the box.
GAF Timberline HDZ has 33 colors across the HD and Bold Definition collections. Owens Corning Duration has 19 on the TruDefinition platform. For homeowners who prioritize color choice, GAF wins.

faq_items.q: Is the SureNail technology worth it?
faq_items.a: Yes, if your contractor installs Owens Corning regularly. The triple-layer nailing strip is the install confidence-builder OC marketing emphasizes. In practice, it slightly reduces blow-off risk and is preferred by contractors who have learned its placement.

faq_items.q: Which shingle does my insurance prefer?
faq_items.a: Insurance cares about wind rating and hail rating, not brand. Both are 130 mph and Class 3 hail. For Class 4 impact discounts, you would need Duration FLEX (Owens Corning) or a similar impact-rated variant; GAF Timberline AS II covers this need in some markets.
